summary: Scorching Scarlet>Roses and a fire‑filled Dark Star/China Doll
blurb: The Missoula show delivers a searing Scarlet Begonias into It Must Have Been The Roses, a tight Weather Report Suite and a blistering Dark Star > China Doll stretch. Jerry’s guitar is front‑and‑center while the rhythm section drives an energetic second set. Recommended for anyone seeking classic ’74 energy with stellar soundboard fidelity.
review: May 14, 1974 at Adams Field House in Missoula captured one of the Dead’s most electrifying performances of the era. The first set rolls solidly through classics before exploding on Scarlet Begonias → It Must Have Been The Roses, where Keith’s piano shines and Jerry “hammering away” on guitar lifts the band into a high‑octane groove that carries straight into a powerful Playing In The Band. The second set is pure momentum: U.S. Blues launches a seamless segue through Weather Report Suite Prelude/Part 1 into Let It Grow, then erupts into a Dark Star → China Doll marathon that fans describe as “smoking” and “on fire.” Row Jimmy, Not Fade Away > Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad and the encore One More Saturday Night round out a night where every member was locked in, with Bill’s drumming described as marching‑band power and Keith’s keys shining throughout. The crisp, excellent soundboard capture makes this historic Montana show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are uniformly positive, praising soaring guitar work, tight rhythm section, and standout sequences (Scarlet>Roses, Dark Star > China Doll). Multiple reviewers use emphatic language like “smoking,” “scorching,” and “on fire,” indicating strong consensus of exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, excellent soundboard capture with front‑and‑center guitar and balanced mix; preserves the high‑octane energy of the show without dropouts.

key_highlights: Smoking Dark Star → China Doll stretch that showcases Jerry’s soaring lead.
Scorching Scarlet Begonias into It Must Have Been The Roses with Keith’s shining piano.
Powerful Playing In The Band and Let It Grow jam that drives the second set momentum.
Seamless Weather Report Suite transition feeding into high‑energy improvisation.
song_highlights: It Must Have Been The Roses

band_performance.Jerry: Inspired, soaring guitar work dominates the mix; his solo on Deal is hailed as one of the best ever, and he’s consistently front‑and‑center throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid low‑frequency foundation with tight bass lines; backing vocals add depth to the overall sound.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers strong rhythm guitar and vocal work; memorable moment when a thrown plastic pitcher hits him during the encore.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s piano and Moog shine, especially on Scarlet Begonias, Weather Report Suite, and improvisational passages, adding a luminous texture.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are described as “on fire” and marching‑band power, driving the set with crisp precision, particularly during Playing In The Band.

must_listen_sequences: It Must Have Been The Roses
Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
